signals.message: You are the Backend Engineer for seeneyu. Your first milestone is M7: implement the full authentication system using NextAuth.js v4 with email+password credentials, role-based access (learner vs admin), middleware-protected /admin/* routes, and sign up / sign in pages. Follow your CLAUDE.md session protocol. Read the project state, then start from Step 1 of M7.

New milestone M10: Script-Aware Coaching Loop. Fix the broken 'Submit for AI Feedback' feature AND add script/character context to the practice + feedback experience. Full spec below.

## ROOT CAUSE OF BUG
The feedback API (src/app/api/sessions/[id]/feedback/route.ts) returns HTTP 422 if `frameUrls` is null or empty. For short recordings, no frames are captured (frame timer fires every 5s, so a <5s practice has 0 frames). Fix: capture 1 frame at 1s after recording starts (already in code but only pushes if framesRef.current.length === 0 — this is correct, but also add a fallback in the API: if 0 frames available, call GPT-4o with text-only — no images — using model gpt-4o-mini with just the prompt text).

## TASK 1 — Schema: Add script field
In prisma/schema.prisma, add to Clip model:
  script  String?  @db.Text

Then run: npm run db:push

## TASK 2 — Types: Add steps to FeedbackResult
In src/lib/types.ts, add to FeedbackResult:
  steps: { number: number; action: string; why: string }[]

## TASK 3 — Enhance AI prompt
Update buildPrompt() in feedback/route.ts:
- Pass characterName, actorName, movieTitle, sceneDescription, and script into the prompt
- Prompt context should say: 'The learner was mimicking [characterName] ([actorName]) from [movieTitle]. The script/dialogue they were asked to perform: [script]. Scene: [sceneDescription].'
- Add to the JSON response schema: "steps": [ { "number": 1, "action": "<specific physical action>", "why": "<1 sentence reason>" }, ... ] (3-5 steps, ordered by priority)
- Also fix the no-frames fallback: if frameUrls.length === 0, send text-only prompt to gpt-4o (no image_url content blocks). Still return valid FeedbackResult.

## TASK 4 — Record page: character banner + script panel
Wait for Designer to deliver UI specs (.shared/outputs/design/m10-spec.md) before implementing these UI components. Designer is working in parallel — their spec will arrive in your signal queue. Once you have it:
- record/page.tsx: fetch clip including characterName, actorName, movieTitle, script. Pass to RecordClient.
- RecordClient.tsx: add CharacterBanner (top of page, above webcam) and ScriptPanel (below annotations checklist) per Designer spec.

## TASK 5 — Feedback page: Action Plan section
In feedback/[sessionId]/page.tsx FeedbackDisplay:
- Add an 'Action Plan' section between the positives/improvements grid and the AI Tips row
- Display feedback.steps as numbered cards: step number, action text, why text
- Use Designer spec for styling.

## TASK 6 — Update Admin CMS ClipForm for script field
In src/app/admin/clips/new/page.tsx and src/app/admin/clips/[id]/edit/page.tsx:
- Add a 'Script / Dialogue' textarea field for the new script column
- This also fixes BUG-001 scope (while you're in the form, fix score dimension ranges 1-10 → 1-3 at the same time)

## TASK 7 — Signal Data Engineer
After db:push is done and schema is live, write a signal to data-engineer.json telling them the script field is ready and they can add script text to the 15 clips in the seed file.

## After completion
Signal Tester (review-request) and PM (task-complete).

M11: Observation Guide — pre-practice clip analysis. Start immediately (parallel with Designer who is producing specs).

## TASK 1 — Schema: observationGuide field
Add to Clip model in prisma/schema.prisma:
  observationGuide Json?  // array of { atSecond, technique, what, why }
Run: npm run db:push

## TASK 2 — Types: ObservationGuide
In src/lib/types.ts add:
  export interface ObservationMoment { atSecond: number; technique: string; what: string; why: string }
  export interface ObservationGuide { moments: ObservationMoment[]; headline: string }
Add observationGuide: ObservationGuide | null to Clip interface.

## TASK 3 — Admin: AI generation route
Create: src/app/api/admin/clips/[id]/observation/route.ts
POST handler: given clipId, call GPT-4o with the clip's annotation, sceneDescription, characterName, and script. Prompt: generate an ObservationGuide JSON with 4-6 moments identifying specific techniques used (timestamp, name, what the character does, why it works). Save to clip.observationGuide.

## TASK 4 — Component: ObservationGuide.tsx
Create: src/components/ObservationGuide.tsx
Read Designer spec from .shared/outputs/design/m11-spec.md (Designer is writing this in parallel — check your signal queue for their delivery signal before finalizing the component).
Core: vertical timeline list. Each item: timestamp badge, technique name, what text, why text.
Graceful: if observationGuide is null, show 'Analysis coming soon' placeholder.

## TASK 5 — Integrate into clip detail page
In src/app/library/[clipId]/page.tsx:
Fetch clip including observationGuide field.
Add a tabbed layout below the YouTube embed: 'Watch' tab (current view) | 'How It Works' tab (ObservationGuide component).

## TASK 6 — Signal Data Engineer
After db:push, signal data-engineer.json that the observationGuide field is in schema and they can add curated observation_guide data to clips-seed.json for all 15 clips.

## After completion: signal Tester + PM.

M12: Micro-Practice Steps (Duolingo-style). BLOCKED until M11 is complete. Start planning, implement after M11 ships.

## Context
User feedback: practice must be split into small steps so the learner can build skill incrementally before doing the full performance. Each step focuses on ONE skill element. Max 30s recording per step. Instant feedback per step.

## TASK 1 — Schema
Add to prisma/schema.prisma:
  model PracticeStep {
    id            String   @id @default(cuid())
    clipId        String
    clip          Clip     @relation(fields: [clipId], references: [id])
    stepNumber    Int
    skillFocus    String   // e.g. 'Eye Contact Duration', 'Vocal Pause'
    instruction   String   @db.Text // what to do in this step
    tip           String?  @db.Text // coaching tip for this step
    targetDurationSec Int  @default(20)
  }
  model MicroSession {
    id           String   @id @default(cuid())
    sessionId    String?  // parent full session if any
    clipId       String
    stepNumber   Int
    recordingUrl String?
    frameUrls    String?  @db.Text
    audioUrl     String?  // for Whisper transcription
    transcript   String?  @db.Text
    feedback     Json?
    status       String   @default('recording')
    createdAt    DateTime @default(now())
  }
Run: npm run db:push

## TASK 2 — Types
Add PracticeStep, MicroFeedback types to src/lib/types.ts.

## TASK 3 — API: micro-sessions
Create: src/app/api/micro-sessions/route.ts (POST: create micro session, upload recording)
Create: src/app/api/micro-sessions/[id]/feedback/route.ts (POST: analyze step recording)
Feedback prompt should be highly focused: only score the ONE skill element for this step (e.g. 'Only evaluate Eye Contact Duration — ignore everything else'). Return pass (score ≥ 7/10) or needs-work.
For vocal-pacing + confident-disagreement clips: transcribe audio via Whisper (openai.audio.transcriptions) before sending to GPT-4o. Include transcript in the analysis prompt.

## TASK 4 — Practice Steps data
After db:push, signal data-engineer.json to add practice_steps (3-5 per clip) to clips-seed.json.

## TASK 5 — UI: Micro-Practice Flow
Create: src/app/library/[clipId]/practice/page.tsx — server page, fetches clip + practiceSteps
Create: src/components/MicroPracticeFlow.tsx — stepper UI per Designer spec (m12-spec.md)
Create: src/components/MicroRecorder.tsx — recorder with 30s countdown ring, captures frames + audio blob
On the clip detail page: change 'Record Practice' button to route to /practice instead of /record.

## After completion: signal Tester + PM. Also signal Data Engineer to add practice_steps seed data.

M13: Onboarding Assessment & Personalized Learning Path. BLOCKED until M12 is complete. Brief for planning:

## Concept
New users complete a 5-skill screening when they sign up. The system scores their baseline level per skill category. A personalized learning path (ordered clip sequence) is generated. Dashboard shows their path + progress.

## TASK 1 — Schema
  model SkillBaseline {
    id           String @id @default(cuid())
    userId       String @unique
    user         User   @relation(fields: [userId], references: [id])
    eyeContact   String // 'beginner'|'intermediate'|'advanced'
    openPosture  String
    activeListening String
    vocalPacing  String
    confidentDisagreement String
    assessedAt   DateTime @default(now())
  }
  model LearningPath {
    id       String @id @default(cuid())
    userId   String
    user     User   @relation(fields: [userId], references: [id])
    entries  Json   // array of { clipId, skillCategory, order, completed, completedAt }
    generatedAt DateTime @default(now())
    updatedAt   DateTime @updatedAt
  }

## TASK 2 — Onboarding flow
Create: src/app/onboarding/ — multi-step assessment (5 screens)
Self-rating per skill + optional 10s sample recording per skill.
POST to /api/onboarding/assessment → stores SkillBaseline + generates LearningPath.

## TASK 3 — Learning path algorithm
Create: src/app/api/onboarding/learning-path/route.ts
Algorithm: for each skill, select clips at the user's assessed level first, then next level. Order by replicationDifficulty ASC. Interleave skills so the path stays varied.

## TASK 4 — Dashboard
Create: src/app/dashboard/page.tsx
Show 5 skill columns with level + progress + next recommended clip card.

## After completion: signal Tester + PM.

SESSION END 2026-03-23. Outstanding work this session — M10, M11, M12, M13 all complete. Next session: (1) Standby for Tester sign-off feedback — fix any issues they file. (2) Once Tester approves, signal Builder to deploy latest commits. (3) Watch for prisma generate EPERM issue on Windows — Vercel postinstall handles it in production. (4) M13 learning path: AI video scoring component (60% weight) is scaffolded but currently uses self-report only — this is a future improvement item when enough session data exists.

BUG-001 (medium) assigned to you. ClipForm score dimension fields in the Admin CMS allow values 1-10, but the model (difficulty score dimensions) expects 1-3. Fix the input field constraints in the admin clip create/edit form to limit score inputs to range 1-3. Reference: .shared/outputs/bugs/bug-001.json. After fixing, signal Tester for verification.
PM context window is at ~90% — session restart is imminent.

M10 UI specs are complete and ready for implementation. File: .shared/outputs/design/m10-spec.md

Three components specified:
1. CharacterBanner — compact horizontal banner above webcam. Shows character name, actor, movie title, and skill category icon. Props: characterName, actorName, movieTitle, skillCategory.
2. ScriptPanel — card below observation checklist. Two modes: 'dialogue' (blockquote style) or 'action' (numbered list). Props: type, content, label, tip. Renders only when clip.script is non-null.
3. ActionPlan — numbered step list on feedback page, between improvements grid and AI Tips. Props: steps[] (action + why per step). Render only when feedbackResult.steps is non-empty.

Key implementation notes in spec:
- ActionPlan left accent border: use absolute positioned element, NOT border-l-4
- Stagger animation: inline style animationDelay per step index
- Add fade-in-up keyframe to tailwind.config.js (spec includes exact config)
- All design tokens align with existing design system (amber accent, bg-surface, etc.)

M11, M12, and M13 UI specs all delivered. Implement in order (M11 → M12 → M13) as per blocking dependencies.

## M11 — Observation Guide (.shared/outputs/design/m11-spec.md)
Components: ObservationTabBar (Watch | How It Works tabs on clip detail page) + ObservationGuide (vertical technique timeline with timestamp badges, what/why per entry). Graceful null state included. Admin CMS needs a 'Generate Observation Guide' button — use existing admin card patterns.

## M12 — Micro-Practice Stepper (.shared/outputs/design/m12-spec.md)
Components: PracticeStepperLayout (top bar + progress bar + dots), StepCard (instruction + tip + jump link), PracticeRecorder (30s countdown ring SVG spec included — ring turns amber → orange → red as time runs out), MicroFeedbackCard (pass/needs-work, slides up with animate-slide-up keyframe), PerformanceUnlockScreen (celebration after all steps). New keyframe 'slide-up' to add to tailwind.config.js is in the spec.

## M13 — Onboarding Assessment & Learning Path (.shared/outputs/design/m13-spec.md)
Part A: 5-screen OnboardingShell + AssessmentSkillScreen (3-button level selector: Beginner/Intermediate/Advanced) + ProcessingScreen + OnboardingCompleteScreen.
Part B: LearningPathDashboard (5-column grid) + SkillTrackColumn + SkillProgressBar + LearningPathCard. YouTube thumbnail URL pattern included: https://i.ytimg.com/vi/[VIDEO_ID]/mqdefault.jpg

All specs include props interfaces, full Tailwind class lists, ASCII mockups, accessibility notes, and dev implementation notes.

NEW MILESTONE M14: Content Expansion — 100+ Lessons from Screenplay Films.

## YOUR TASK
Three things to do for M14:

### TASK 1 — Schema: Add screenplaySource field
In prisma/schema.prisma, add to Clip model:
  screenplaySource  String?

Run: npm run db:push && npx prisma generate

Signal data-engineer when db:push is done so they know the field exists.

### TASK 2 — Admin CMS: Add screenplaySource input
In src/app/admin/clips/new/page.tsx and src/app/admin/clips/[id]/edit/page.tsx:
- Add a "Screenplay Source URL" text input field for screenplaySource
- Place it after the Script textarea

### TASK 3 — Library: Film filter + screenplay badge
In src/app/library/page.tsx:
- Add "Filter by Film" dropdown (derive unique movieTitle values from clips)
- Add "Has Screenplay" checkbox filter (shows only clips where screenplaySource is not null)

In src/components/ClipCard.tsx:
- Show a small amber badge "Screenplay" when clip.screenplaySource is set

In src/app/library/[clipId]/page.tsx (clip detail):
- Show a "Read Screenplay" link button when screenplaySource is set (opens in new tab)

Wait for Designer to deliver M14 UI spec (.shared/outputs/design/m14-spec.md) for exact styling of the filter and badge.

### After M14 completion
Signal PM and Tester.

NEW MILESTONE M15: Performing Foundation Section (separate from library/practice).

## OVERVIEW
Build a structured learning section at /foundation — completely separate from the clip practice section. Think Udemy: courses → lessons → theory + examples + quiz. Three courses: Voice Mastery, Verbal Communication, Body Language Fundamentals.

## TASK 1 — Schema: New Foundation Models
Add to prisma/schema.prisma:

model FoundationCourse {
  id          String            @id @default(cuid())
  slug        String            @unique
  title       String
  description String
  icon        String
  color       String
  order       Int
  lessons     FoundationLesson[]
  createdAt   DateTime          @default(now())
}

model FoundationLesson {
  id          String              @id @default(cuid())
  courseId    String
  course      FoundationCourse    @relation(fields: [courseId], references: [id])
  slug        String
  title       String
  theoryHtml  String              @db.Text
  order       Int
  examples    LessonExample[]
  questions   QuizQuestion[]
  progress    FoundationProgress[]
  @@unique([courseId, slug])
}

model LessonExample {
  id          String           @id @default(cuid())
  lessonId    String
  lesson      FoundationLesson @relation(fields: [lessonId], references: [id])
  youtubeId   String
  title       String
  description String
  startTime   Int?
}

model QuizQuestion {
  id           String           @id @default(cuid())
  lessonId     String
  lesson       FoundationLesson @relation(fields: [lessonId], references: [id])
  question     String
  options      Json
  correctIndex Int
  explanation  String
  order        Int              @default(0)
}

model FoundationProgress {
  id          String           @id @default(cuid())
  userId      String
  user        User             @relation(fields: [userId], references: [id])
  lessonId    String
  lesson      FoundationLesson @relation(fields: [lessonId], references: [id])
  quizScore   Int?
  quizPassed  Boolean          @default(false)
  completedAt DateTime?
  @@unique([userId, lessonId])
}

Add FoundationProgress[] to User model.

Run: npm run db:push && npx prisma generate

## TASK 2 — API Routes
src/app/api/foundation/progress/route.ts — POST: mark lesson complete, save quiz score
src/app/api/foundation/progress/[lessonId]/route.ts — GET: get user progress for a lesson

## TASK 3 — Pages (wait for Designer spec in .shared/outputs/design/m15-spec.md)
src/app/foundation/page.tsx — Course catalog: 3 course cards with title, description, icon, lesson count, user progress % 
src/app/foundation/[courseSlug]/page.tsx — Course page: lesson list with completion checkmarks, lesson order
src/app/foundation/[courseSlug]/[lessonSlug]/page.tsx — Lesson page:
  - Section 1: Theory (render theoryHtml safely with dangerouslySetInnerHTML, styled with typography)
  - Section 2: Examples (YouTube embeds with title + description)
  - Section 3: Quiz (MCQ cards, one at a time or all at once, submit → show score + explanations)
  - Section 4: Navigation (Prev Lesson / Next Lesson buttons)
  - Progress auto-saved when quiz is submitted

## TASK 4 — NavBar
Add "Foundation" link to src/components/NavBar.tsx (next to Library)

## TASK 5 — Seed
Wait for data-engineer to deliver .shared/outputs/data/foundation-curriculum.json.
Update prisma/seed.ts to seed all FoundationCourse, FoundationLesson, LessonExample, QuizQuestion records.

## After completion
Signal Tester and PM.

M14 and M15 UI specs delivered. m14-spec.md covers: Library Film filter dropdown, Screenplay toggle filter, ClipCard screenplay badge (top-right thumbnail), clip detail screenplay link. m15-spec.md covers: course catalog (3 cards), course page (lesson list), lesson page (theory/examples/quiz) with all Tailwind classes and interactive states.

NEW MILESTONE M16: Learning Materials Builder — Backend Implementation.

WAIT for Designer to deliver m16-spec.md (.shared/outputs/design/m16-spec.md) before building admin pages. Start schema + services + API immediately.

## TASK 1 — Schema: CrawlJob + CrawlResult
Add to prisma/schema.prisma:

  model CrawlJob {
    id            String       @id @default(cuid())
    name          String
    skillCategory String       // standard category OR custom tactic name
    technique     String?      // specific named tactic, e.g. 'Triangle Gaze'
    keywords      Json         // String[] of YouTube search terms
    difficulty    String?      // 'beginner' | 'intermediate' | 'advanced' | 'all'
    maxResults    Int          @default(20)
    status        String       @default('pending') // pending | running | complete | failed
    errorMessage  String?      @db.Text
    createdBy     String       // admin userId
    createdAt     DateTime     @default(now())
    updatedAt     DateTime     @updatedAt
    results       CrawlResult[]
    @@index([status])
    @@index([createdBy])
  }

  model CrawlResult {
    id               String    @id @default(cuid())
    jobId            String
    job              CrawlJob  @relation(fields: [jobId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
    youtubeId        String
    title            String
    channelName      String
    thumbnailUrl     String
    description      String    @db.Text
    durationSec      Int?
    publishedAt      String?
    viewCount        Int?
    relevanceScore   Float     @default(0) // 0-10 from GPT-4o
    aiAnalysis       String?   @db.Text    // why this matches the criteria
    status           String    @default('pending') // pending | approved | rejected
    approvedClipId   String?   // link to Clip if approved
    createdAt        DateTime  @default(now())
    @@index([jobId])
    @@index([jobId, status])
  }

Run: npm run db:push && npx prisma generate

## TASK 2 — Types
In src/lib/types.ts add:
  CrawlJob, CrawlResult, CrawlJobStatus, CrawlResultStatus types

## TASK 3 — YouTube Crawler Service
Create: src/services/youtube-crawler.ts
- Uses YOUTUBE_API_KEY from env
- Function: searchYouTube(query: string, maxResults: number) → YouTubeSearchResult[]
- YouTube Data API v3 endpoint: https://www.googleapis.com/youtube/v3/search
- Params: q, part=snippet, type=video, maxResults, relevantLanguage=en, safeSearch=strict
- Also call videos endpoint for duration: https://www.googleapis.com/youtube/v3/videos?part=contentDetails,statistics
- Return: { youtubeId, title, channelName, thumbnailUrl, description, durationSec, publishedAt, viewCount }

## TASK 4 — Relevance Scorer Service
Create: src/services/clip-relevance-scorer.ts
- Function: scoreClipRelevance(result: YouTubeSearchResult, criteria: JobCriteria) → { score: number, analysis: string }
- Call GPT-4o (gpt-4o-mini for cost efficiency) with:
  - Criteria: skill category, technique, target difficulty
  - Video: title, description, channel name
  - Prompt: 'Rate this YouTube video 0-10 for usefulness as a communication skill training clip. Criteria: [criteria]. Video: [title] by [channel]. [description]. Return JSON: { score: 0-10, analysis: "1-2 sentence reason" }'
- Batch scoring: process results in parallel (Promise.all with rate limit)

## TASK 5 — API Routes
Create: src/app/api/admin/crawl-jobs/route.ts
  GET: list all jobs (paginated, sorted by createdAt desc)
  POST: create job, status=pending, createdBy from session

Create: src/app/api/admin/crawl-jobs/[id]/route.ts
  GET: job detail + results

Create: src/app/api/admin/crawl-jobs/[id]/run/route.ts
  POST: execute job
    1. Set job status=running
    2. Build YouTube query from job.keywords + job.technique + job.skillCategory
    3. Call youtube-crawler.ts → get candidates
    4. For each result: call clip-relevance-scorer.ts
    5. Save all CrawlResult records to DB (sorted by relevanceScore desc)
    6. Set job status=complete
    Error handling: set status=failed + errorMessage

Create: src/app/api/admin/crawl-jobs/[id]/results/[resultId]/route.ts
  PATCH: body { action: 'approve' | 'reject', clipMetadata?: {...} }
    - reject: set result.status=rejected
    - approve: create Clip record from clipMetadata + result data, set result.status=approved + approvedClipId
      After creating Clip: automatically POST to /api/admin/clips/[clipId]/observation to generate ObservationGuide
      Then signal Data Engineer to add practice_steps (or auto-generate them)

## TASK 6 — Admin Pages (wait for m16-spec.md from Designer)
Create:
  src/app/admin/crawl-jobs/page.tsx — job list
  src/app/admin/crawl-jobs/new/page.tsx — create form
  src/app/admin/crawl-jobs/[id]/page.tsx — job detail + results browser
Add 'Learning Materials' link to admin sidebar/nav.

## TASK 7 — Env var
Requires YOUTUBE_API_KEY in .env.local and Vercel env vars.
Document in a signal to Builder after implementation.

## After completion
Signal Tester + PM.

M16 communication tactics taxonomy ready.

File: .shared/outputs/data/communication-tactics.json

Stats:
- 50 tactics total
- Domains: vocal(10), verbal(16), body(12), presence(5), rapport(7)
- Difficulty: beginner(16), intermediate(24), advanced(10)
- Each tactic has: id, name, domain, description, difficulty, searchKeywords (3-5), scoringHints

The searchKeywords are designed for YouTube Data API v3 searches in the crawl job system. The scoringHints are for the GPT-4o relevance scorer to identify good vs poor examples.

M16 UI spec delivered. Full spec at: .shared/outputs/design/m16-spec.md

Covers all 4 views with ASCII mockups, Tailwind classes, and component breakdown:
1. Job List (/admin/crawl-jobs) — JobListRow with status chips (pending/running/complete/failed), Run/View Results buttons, result count stats
2. Create Job Form (/admin/crawl-jobs/new) — KeywordsTagInput (tag chips, Enter to add), Skill dropdown with Special Technique conditional field, difficulty pill group, Create vs Create & Run submit buttons
3. Job Detail + Results Browser (/admin/crawl-jobs/[id]) — 4-step run progress indicator, ResultCard grid (3-col desktop), relevance score badge (green ≥7 / amber 4-6 / red <4), optimistic approve/reject with undo
4. Approve Modal (overlay) — 2-panel: YouTube embed left + metadata form right, 'Create Clip & Generate Guide' CTA, success toast

Component suggestions: JobListRow, KeywordsTagInput, ResultCard, ResultsGrid, JobRunProgress, ApproveModal. All in src/components/admin/.
